steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: 'C. Maxx Stevens is a Seminole/Muscogee artist whose body of ______ interdisciplinary, encompassing sculpture, printmaking, and installation...' The relative clause introduced by 'whose' needs a finite main verb to be grammatically complete; 'interdisciplinary' is a predicate adjective that must attach to that verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'work, having been': 'Having been' is a perfect participle — a non-finite form that cannot serve as the main predicate of the relative clause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'work is': 'Is' is a finite verb; 'whose body of work is interdisciplinary' is a complete, grammatical relative clause with 'is' as the predicate and 'interdisciplinary' as the predicate adjective. ✓
  • Choice C — 'work, to be': An infinitive cannot function as the main predicate of a relative clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'work being': 'Being' without an auxiliary is a participle, not a finite verb, so the clause has no proper predicate. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
